design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate themed stages collecting five large coins per level while rescuing friends and avoiding enemies; defeat final boss Switchblade Sam
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Time pressure (999 seconds per stage) combined with health management and collectible completionism
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Play as the mischievous Dennis Mitchell protecting his town and friends from a burglar threat
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Strict time limits with no reset per death create harsh progression gating; mandatory full-level restart on timeout regardless of progress; collectible-based victory condition (both friends AND full coin collection required) emphasizes thoroughness over speed

difficulty.lives_system: Hearts-based health system; one heart lost per enemy touch with temporary stun; complete heart depletion restarts current level only
difficulty.continues_system: No explicit continues system documented; level restart on death retains collected coins but resets timer
difficulty.checkpointing: Checkpoint per stage; if time runs out, complete level progress is erased regardless of coins collected
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Limited. Difficulty is fixed; player can manage risk via candy collection for health recovery, but time pressure and enemy patterns appear non-adjustable. Reviewers noted 'fiddly and slow' gameplay suggesting mechanical difficulty rather than player-controlled scaling

progression.unlock_mechanism: Stage-based linear progression: Mr. Wilson's house → great outdoors → boiler room → final boss encounter with Switchblade Sam
replayability_hooks: Collectible completionism: must find both friends (Joey and Margaret) AND entire coin collection to achieve full victory
Health/resource management optimization within strict time windows
Mixed reviews suggest replayability may be limited due to 'unappealing' character execution and 'slow' gameplay

world_building_techniques: Thematic stage design (Mr. Wilson's house, outdoors, boiler room connecting to final confrontation)
Narrative context via stage progression: rescue friends + retrieve coin collection, culminating in burglar confrontation
Character-driven motivation (Dennis as protagonist protecting his community and elderly neighbor)

boss_design.philosophy: Single narrative antagonist (Switchblade Sam from 1993 film) serves as climactic stage finale
notable_bosses: Switchblade Sam - final stage antagonist, burglar who accessed town via railroad; serves as campaign conclusion

faction_indicators: Dennis Mitchell (protagonist) vs. Switchblade Sam (antagonist burglar)
Community protection angle: rescuing friends Joey and Margaret, protecting Mr. Wilson's property
danger_signals: Burglar threat (Switchblade Sam) arriving via infrastructure (railroad connection)
Enemy encounters throughout stages designed to obstruct player progress
environmental_storytelling: Mr. Wilson's house as starting location establishes elderly neighbor relationship
Progression through 'great outdoors' and 'boiler room' creates geographic narrative of pursuit
Coin collection objective ties to protecting wealth/property from theft
